data.title: Kevin Warsh's Confirmation as Federal Reserve Chair Gains Momentum
data.description: The probability of Kevin Warsh being confirmed as the Federal Reserve Chair by May 15 has surged from 27% to 85%, according to Odaily. This increase is attributed to the U.S. Department of Justice's expected decision to drop the criminal investigation against current Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell. Previously, Thom Tillis, a member of the Senate Banking Committee, stated he would block Warsh's nomination from reaching a full Senate vote if U.S. President Donald Trump did not withdraw the investigation against Powell. With the anticipated withdrawal of the investigation, the market now expects Warsh to be confirmed by the Senate before Powell's term ends on May 15.

data.title: JPX Considers Rule Change for Index Inclusion of Cryptoasset Companies
data.description: JPX Market Innovation & Research is evaluating a proposed rule that would delay the inclusion of companies primarily holding cryptoassets in TOPIX and other indices subject to periodic review. According to NS3.AI, the proposal has been criticized for being vague, asset-specific, and open-ended. The public comment period for this proposal is set to close on May 7, 2026.

data.title: STOCKS | U.S. Stock Indices Show Mixed Performance
data.description: U.S. stock indices exhibited varied movements recently, with the Nasdaq Composite rising over 1%, the S&P 500 increasing by 0.4%, and the Dow Jones Industrial Average declining by 0.3%. According to Jin10, these fluctuations reflect differing investor sentiments across the major indices.

data.title: Energy Recovery Delayed by Persian Gulf Closure
data.description: The prolonged closure of the Persian Gulf is expected to significantly delay the recovery of energy flows, potentially taking months or even years. Wall Street Journal (Markets) posted on X, highlighting the impact of this closure on global energy markets. The disruption has raised concerns among industry experts about the long-term effects on supply chains and energy prices. As the situation unfolds, stakeholders are closely monitoring developments to assess the potential implications for the global economy.

data.title: The White House is confident the Senate will quickly confirm Kevin Warsh as the next Federal Reserve Chairman.
data.description: The White House is confident that the Senate will quickly confirm Kevin Warsh as the next Federal Reserve Chairman. (Jinshi)

data.title: On Polymarket, the probability that "Wash will be confirmed as the Federal Reserve Chairman before May 15" rose to 85%, a short-term increase of 211%.
data.description: Odaily Seer's monitoring shows that the probability of "Wash being confirmed as Federal Reserve Chairman before May 15th" on Polymarket has risen from 27% to 85%, a short-term increase of 211%. This is due to the U.S. Department of Justice's expected withdrawal of its criminal investigation into Federal Reserve Chairman Powell. Previously, Senator Thom Tillis, a member of the Banking Committee, stated that if Trump did not withdraw the criminal investigation against Powell, he would block Wash's nomination to the full Senate. With the current news of the Justice Department withdrawing its criminal investigation against Powell, the market expects Wash to have a very high probability of being confirmed by the Senate as Federal Reserve Chairman before Powell's term expires (May 15th). Odaily Seer continues to monitor the prediction market, seeing changes before they are priced in.

data.title: The Ethereum Foundation sold 10,000 ETH to Bitmine today.
data.description: The Ethereum Foundation announced on the X platform that it completed the sale of 10,000 ETH today via OTC, at an average price of $2,387. The counterparty in this OTC transaction was Bitmine.

data.title: AI Demand Boosts X-Energy's Valuation Above $9 Billion Ahead of Public Debut
data.description: Nuclear-energy company X-Energy is poised to go public on Friday with a valuation exceeding $9 billion, driven by the increasing demand for electricity fueled by advancements in artificial intelligence. Wall Street Journal (Markets) posted on X that the company's entry into the public market highlights the growing interest in alternative energy sources as AI technologies continue to expand. The heightened need for reliable and sustainable power solutions is pushing companies like X-Energy to the forefront, as they seek to capitalize on the evolving energy landscape. This move underscores the intersection of technology and energy sectors, as AI's rapid growth necessitates innovative approaches to meet its substantial energy requirements.

data.title: Ethereum Foundation Sells 20,000 ETH Since March 15
data.description: The Ethereum Foundation has reportedly sold a total of 20,000 ETH since March 15, according to analyst @ai_9684xtpa posted on X. Five minutes ago, the Foundation's DeFi address transferred 10,000 ETH, valued at $23.13 million, to a new address. The Foundation's official account announced that this portion of tokens was sold via an over-the-counter (OTC) transaction, with the buyer being the Bitmine wallet address.

data.title: Crypto Firms Urge SEC for Formal DeFi Rules
data.description:
A coalition of over 20 cryptocurrency companies and organizations has urged the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) to formalize its guidance on decentralized finance (DeFi) interfaces into official regulations. According to NS3.AI, the group, spearheaded by the DeFi Education Fund, argues that informal guidance lacks the necessary long-term regulatory clarity.

This appeal comes in response to recent SEC guidance suggesting that certain DeFi front-ends and wallets might not be subject to broker-dealer regulations. However, industry participants express concern that the definition could be expanded in the future, potentially affecting a broader range of entities.

data.title: U.S. Treasury Secretary Highlights Strengthening Dollar Dominance
data.description: U.S. Treasury Secretary Besant has stated that the dominance of the U.S. dollar and its status as a reserve currency have further strengthened. According to ChainCatcher, this assertion underscores the ongoing influence of the dollar in global financial markets.

data.title: U.S. Consumer Confidence Drops Amid Iran Conflict Concerns
data.description:
On April 24, Jin10 reported that a survey released on Friday indicated a decline in U.S. consumer confidence in April, reaching its lowest level in nearly four years due to inflation concerns exacerbated by the Iran conflict. According to Jin10, the University of Michigan's Consumer Survey Center stated that its consumer confidence index's final reading for the month was 49.8, marking the lowest point since June 2022. However, this figure showed an improvement from the earlier April reading of 47.6. In March, the index stood at 53.3.

"Following the announcement of a two-week ceasefire agreement and a slight decrease in gasoline prices, consumer confidence has partially recovered from the early month's decline," said Joanne Hsu, director of the Consumer Survey Center. "The Iran conflict appears to impact consumer perceptions primarily through its effects on gasoline prices and other potential costs. In contrast, military and diplomatic developments that fail to alleviate supply constraints or reduce energy prices are unlikely to boost consumer confidence."

data.title: Ameriprise Data Breach Affects Thousands, Exposing Sensitive Information
data.description: Ameriprise has revealed that a data breach in March impacted 47,876 individuals, potentially compromising their personal and financial information. According to NS3.AI, the company identified the breach on March 18. Additionally, Ameriprise reported a separate phishing incident in December, which affected 598 people.

data.title: Kalibaf Dismisses Resignation Rumors
data.description: Aiman Shamsaei has clarified that Kalibaf has not resigned and continues to perform his responsibilities. According to NS3.AI, Shamsaei stated that the rumors regarding Kalibaf's resignation were circulated to mislead public opinion.

data.title: U.S. Department of Justice to End Investigation into Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell
data.description: The U.S. Department of Justice is anticipated to conclude its criminal investigation concerning Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell. According to NS3.AI, Powell has been identified as the subject of this investigation. The decision to drop the investigation marks a significant development in the scrutiny surrounding Powell's actions and decisions during his tenure. The outcome may influence perceptions of the Federal Reserve's leadership and its ongoing policies.

data.title: The Justice Department is expected to drop the criminal investigation against Federal Reserve Chairman Jerome Powell.
data.description: ABC News: The Justice Department is expected to drop the criminal investigation into Federal Reserve Chairman Jerome Powell.

data.title: China and Indonesia to Launch QRIS Cross-Border Payments on April 30
data.description: China and Indonesia have announced the official launch of QRIS cross-border payments starting April 30, following a successful trial that processed 1.64 million transactions valued at Rp 556 billion ($32 million). According to NS3.AI, Bank Indonesia stated that the system will enable travelers in both countries to use compatible payment apps, reducing reliance on U.S. dollars. BI Deputy Governor Filianingsih Hendarta confirmed that the technical and business aspects are prepared for implementation.

data.title: Strategy Surpasses BlackRock as Largest Institutional Bitcoin Holder
data.description: Strategy announced this week that it now holds over 800,000 Bitcoin following a $2.5 billion acquisition of 34,000 Bitcoin. According to NS3.AI, Michael Saylor, the company's executive chairman, stated that the 'bitcoin winter' has ended, as Bitcoin has rebounded 30% from its recent lows of $60,000. This purchase positions Strategy ahead of BlackRock's IBIT fund, marking the first time since the second quarter of 2024 that Strategy has become the largest institutional Bitcoin holder. Benoît Bosc from x2B commented that the rebound is more indicative of macro liquidity trends rather than crypto-specific strength.

data.title: Iranian Official Denies Resignation of Negotiation Leader
data.description:
Iranian Parliament's Communication, Media, and Cultural Affairs Center head, Ayman Shamsaei, has refuted claims regarding the resignation of the negotiation leader, Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf. According to Odaily, Shamsaei stated that Ghalibaf has not resigned and continues to fulfill his duties diligently. The rumors were spread with the intent to disrupt public opinion.

Earlier reports from Iranian media suggested that Ghalibaf, who was leading the Iranian delegation in talks with the United States in Islamabad, had resigned and withdrawn from the negotiation team.

data.title: Reliance Industries' Q4 Net Profit Surpasses Expectations
data.description: Reliance Industries reported a net profit of 169.7 billion rupees for the fourth quarter, exceeding the anticipated 169.43 billion rupees. According to Jin10, the company's performance was stronger than analysts had forecasted, reflecting its robust financial health. The results highlight Reliance Industries' ability to outperform market expectations, contributing to its reputation as a leading player in the industry.
